file_based_prompts.overview: The askBob helper automatically handles prompt file creation internally, so you pass prompt text directly as a string parameter. The helper creates a temporary file, passes it to Bob, and cleans it up automatically. This approach avoids shell escaping issues and handles complex content like diffs, logs, and multi-line text.

reason.title: Shell Escaping Nightmares
reason.description: Inline prompts on command line require escaping quotes, backticks, dollar signs, newlines, and other special characters. This becomes impossible with complex content.
reason.example_problem: // This will FAIL with shell escaping issues: sh "bob --prompt 'Review this diff:\n${gitDiff}'"

reason.title: Diff and Log Content
reason.description: Git diffs and test logs contain special characters that break shell parsing: - Quotes and backticks in code - Dollar signs in variable names - Angle brackets in generics - Newlines and indentation

reason.title: Readability and Debugging
reason.description: File-based prompts are easier to inspect, debug, and version control. You can cat the prompt file to see exactly what Bob received.

pattern.name: simple_text_prompt
pattern.description: Basic text prompt without dynamic content
pattern.example:
script { def prompt = """ Review the changes in this pull request for: - Code quality and maintainability - Security vulnerabilities - Performance issues - Test coverage """
def analysis = askBob(prompt, 'solution-code-reviewer') echo analysis }

pattern.name: prompt_with_git_diff
pattern.description: Include git diff output in prompt

pattern.name: prompt_with_test_output
pattern.description: Include test failure logs in prompt

pattern.name: prompt_with_multiple_files
prompt_with_context_files
pattern.description: Include relevant source files for context

practice.title: Use Triple-Quoted Strings for Prompts
practice.description: Use Groovy triple-quoted strings (""") for multi-line prompts. The askBob helper handles file creation internally.
practice.good: def prompt = """ This is a multi-line prompt. It can contain "quotes" and 'apostrophes'. Even ${variables} work correctly. """ def analysis = askBob(prompt, 'mode-slug')

practice.title: Capture and Use Return Value
practice.description: Always capture askBob's return value to display, process, or archive the analysis.

practice.title: No Manual File Cleanup Needed
practice.description: The askBob helper automatically creates and cleans up temporary prompt files. You don't need to manage these files yourself.
practice.note: The helper creates files like .bob-prompt-{timestamp}.txt and removes them after use

practice.title: Limit Content Size
practice.description: Be mindful of prompt size. For large diffs or logs, include only relevant portions or summarize.

pitfall.name: not_capturing_return_value
pitfall.bad: // DON'T DO THIS - analysis is lost askBob(prompt, 'solution-code-reviewer')

pitfall.name: inline_command_line_prompt
pitfall.description: Trying to call bob CLI directly
pitfall.bad: // DON'T DO THIS - bypasses the helper sh "bob --chat-mode solution-code-reviewer -p 'Review this'"
pitfall.good: // DO THIS - use the helper def analysis = askBob("Review this", 'solution-code-reviewer')

pitfall.name: not_reading_file_content
pitfall.description: Forgetting to read file content before including in prompt
pitfall.bad: // This includes the filename, not the content! def prompt = "Analyze: ${WORKSPACE}/test.log"

issue.name: prompt_empty_or_truncated
issue.symptom: Bob receives empty or truncated prompt
issue.solution: - Add echo statements to verify prompt content - Check that variables are populated before building prompt - Use triple-quoted strings for multi-line content

issue.name: analysis_not_visible
issue.symptom: Bob runs but output not visible
issue.cause: Not capturing or displaying the return value
issue.solution: - Capture: def analysis = askBob(...) - Display: echo analysis - Archive: writeFile file: 'output.txt', text: analysis

issue.name: prompt_too_large
issue.symptom: Bob times out or fails with large prompts
issue.solution: - Limit diff to changed files only - Take tail of logs instead of full content - Summarize or filter content before including - Split into multiple smaller prompts if needed

quick_reference.command: writeFile file: 'output.txt', text: analysis // Save for archiving
def content = readFile("file.txt") // Read file to include in prompt
